title: Bank of England Holds Interest Rates at 5.25%
excerpt: The Bank of England has held interest rates at 5.25% for the third consecutive meeting as inflation continues to ease.
content: The Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee voted to maintain the base rate at 5.25% in its latest meeting. This decision comes as UK inflation has fallen to 3.4% - the lowest level in two years.

title: UK House Prices Rise for Third Consecutive Month
excerpt: UK house prices have risen for the third month in a row according to the latest Nationwide index.
content: Average UK house prices rose by 0.7% in February to reach £260
status: 420 - marking the third consecutive monthly increase. The annual rate of change now stands at -1.2%.

title: Stamp Duty Holiday Extension Rumours Dismissed
excerpt: The Treasury has dismissed rumours of a further stamp duty holiday extension ahead of the Spring Budget.
content: A Treasury spokesperson confirmed there are no plans to extend the current stamp duty thresholds beyond March 2025. Buyers are urged to plan completions accordingly.

title: Rightmove Reports Surge in Spring Property Listings
excerpt: Rightmove has reported a significant increase in new property listings as sellers enter the spring market.
content: New seller listings rose 12% year-on-year in February according to Rightmove data. The portal reports the highest stock levels since 2021 giving buyers more choice.

title: Leasehold Reform Bill Passes Third Reading
excerpt: The Leasehold and Freehold Reform Bill has passed its third reading in the House of Commons.
content: The legislation will give millions of leaseholders new rights including easier and cheaper routes to extend leases and buy freeholds. It also bans new leasehold houses.

title: First-Time Buyer Numbers Hit Five-Year High
excerpt: First-time buyer completions have reached their highest level in five years according to Halifax data.
status: 400 first-time buyers purchased a home in 2023 - an increase of 11% from 2022 and the highest number since 2018.

title: Help to Buy ISA Deadline Approaches
excerpt: Savers with Help to Buy ISAs have until November 2029 to use their bonus when buying their first home.
content: The government has confirmed the deadline for claiming the Help to Buy ISA bonus remains November 2029. Savers must have opened their account before the scheme closed in 2019.

title: Rental Demand Outstrips Supply in Major Cities
excerpt: New data shows rental demand continues to significantly outstrip supply in London
status: Zoopla's latest market report reveals rental demand is 42% above the five-year average while supply remains 26% below. Average rents have risen 9.7% year-on-year nationwide.

title: Green Mortgages: What Are They and Should You Get One
excerpt: Green mortgages offer lower interest rates for energy-efficient homes - here's everything you need to know.
content: Green mortgages are increasingly popular as lenders reward buyers and homeowners who improve the energy efficiency of their properties. This article explains how they work and who qualifies.

title: Property Market Predictions 2024: What Experts Say
excerpt: We asked leading property experts for their predictions on house prices
content: mortgage rates and transaction volumes in 2024.
status: Five leading property economists give their forecasts for the UK housing market in 2024. The consensus points to modest price falls of 2-4% before a recovery begins in H2 2024.
